evals.prompt: I'm running 3 agent chats in parallel right now and I keep losing track of what each one is doing. Can you help me track progress on this current session? I'm working on implementing a payment integration and I want to see where things stand.
evals.expected_output: A ~/.agent-tracker/<id>.json state file with the new schema (id, name, status, tasks object with counts + items, timeline array, errors with type/message/resolved/context). Running generate_dashboard.py should produce fleet.html and agent-<id>.html in ~/.agent-tracker/output/. The agent should explain the multi-agent workflow.

evals.prompt: I have 3 agents running: one doing auth-service, one doing frontend-redesign, and one doing data-migration. Show me the fleet dashboard with all of them.
evals.expected_output: Three JSON files in ~/.agent-tracker/ (auth-service.json, frontend-redesign.json, data-migration.json), each with the new schema. Running the script should generate fleet.html showing all 3 agents in a card grid with metrics, plus 3 agent-*.html detail pages with tabbed interfaces.

evals.prompt: I just finished a big refactoring task and want to create a progress report. The session took about 2 hours, we modified 15 files, encountered 3 errors that were all resolved, and completed all 12 tasks. Generate a dashboard showing this.
evals.expected_output: A complete agent JSON with status 'done', all 12 tasks marked done, all errors with resolved: true, 15 files_changed entries with lines counts, and accurate metrics. The fleet dashboard should show this agent as Completed with green status pill. The detail page should show 100% on all phases.

evals.prompt: One of my agents is blocked on a database migration error and another is still running. Can you track both and make sure the blockers are visible on the fleet dashboard?
evals.expected_output: Two agent JSON files: one with status 'blocked' and an unresolved error (resolved: false), another with status 'running'. The fleet dashboard should show the Blocked metric card with alert styling (red border), and the blocked agent card should have a red left border. The detail page should show the error in the Errors tab as unresolved.

evals.prompt: I want to see a timeline of everything that happened across my auth-service agent session. It had 10 events including some errors and file creations.
evals.expected_output: An agent JSON with a timeline array containing 10 events with time, text, and type fields (accent, success, danger, info). The detail page Timeline tab should render all events in a vertical timeline with colored dots.
